agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"Client","to":"POST /validate","status":"risk","issue":"No request ID propagated or generated — impossible to correlate a client complaint to a specific request through the system"},{"from":"POST /validate","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Single external dependency with 3s timeout but no retry, no circuit breaker, no fallback. A SmartyStreets brownout (slow 200s, intermittent 500s) will cascade directly to callers"},{"from":"/health","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Health check couples liveness to an external dependency. If SmartyStreets has a brief outage, the orchestrator (k8s) will kill healthy pods, turning a partial degradation into a full outage"}],"contract_compliance":"No schema validation details provided for the POST /validate request body. Unknown whether malformed input is rejected with 400 or passes through to SmartyStreets, wasting quota and latency.","data_flow_integrity":"Input address is not logged and raw SmartyStreets response is not logged. When a customer disputes a validation result, there is zero forensic trail to determine what was sent or received."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No request ID / correlation ID in any log line — cannot trace a single request across log aggregation","No input address logged — cannot reproduce or investigate a disputed validation","No raw SmartyStreets response logged (even at DEBUG level) — cannot distinguish between our bug and upstream bug","No duration logged for the SmartyStreets call — cannot detect latency degradation from logs alone","No structured logging apparent — grep-friendly strings but not machine-parseable for dashboards","No log on SmartyStreets call failure (timeout, 5xx, network error) with error details"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Minimal — single counter with no dimensional breakdown","gaps":["validation_requests_total has no status label (valid/invalid/error) — cannot compute error rate or validity ratio","No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds) — cannot set SLO, cannot alert on p99 degradation","No metric for SmartyStreets upstream errors (smartystreets_errors_total) — cannot distinguish our failures from theirs","No in-flight request gauge — cannot detect connection pool exhaustion or thread starvation","No metric for SmartyStreets response time specifically — cannot separate our processing time from upstream time"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"No distributed tracing mentioned","gaps":["No trace context propagation (W3C traceparent / B3 headers)","No span for the SmartyStreets outbound call — invisible in any service mesh or APM tool"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am seeing elevated 5xx rates has: one counter with no breakdown, two generic log lines with no request ID, no way to pull the specific address or API response that caused the failure, and no latency data to confirm whether the issue is upstream or local."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns 5xx or times out","handling":"partial","recommendation":"3s timeout exists (good), but no retry with backoff for transient errors. Add 1 retry with jitter for 5xx/timeout. Add a circuit breaker (e.g. 50% error rate over 30s window) to fail fast during sustained outages instead of blocking threads for 3s each."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets latency degrades (responds in 2.9s consistently)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No latency histogram means this is invisible until the 3s timeout starts firing. Add validation_upstream_duration_seconds histogram and alert on p99 > 2s."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets API key expires or is revoked","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Likely returns 401/403. Without logging the HTTP status or having a metric for auth failures, this will appear as generic errors. Log and meter upstream HTTP status codes."},{"scenario":"Malformed input (empty address, garbage data)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of input validation. Reject obviously bad input with 400 before calling SmartyStreets to save quota and reduce latency. Log validation rejections separately."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets rate limiting (429)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of 429 handling. Should detect and back off, not retry immediately. Expose a metric for rate-limit hits."},{"scenario":"Pod killed during in-flight request due to health check failure","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Separate liveness probe (always 200 if process is up) from readiness probe (check SmartyStreets). This prevents the orchestrator from killing pods during upstream outages."}],"retry_config":"None. Single attempt only. Transient SmartyStreets errors become immediate caller failures.","timeout_config":"3s on SmartyStreets call. Reasonable default, but no evidence of an end-to-end request timeout or caller-facing timeout header.","circuit_breakers":"None. During a sustained SmartyStreets outage, every request will block for up to 3s before failing, consuming threads/connections.","graceful_degradation":"None. No fallback behavior (e.g. return 'unverified' status with a flag, cache recent results, or use a secondary provider)."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous image. Acceptable for a stateless service with no database migrations. Verify zero sticky state (caches, local files) that would make rollback non-trivial.","monitoring_coverage":"Insufficient. Cannot compute error rate, cannot measure latency, cannot distinguish upstream vs local failures, cannot alert on degradation before customers notice.","on_call_documentation":"No runbook mentioned. On-call engineer needs at minimum: how to check SmartyStreets status, how to verify API key validity, how to read the one metric that exists, escalation contacts, and manual validation fallback procedure.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add structured logging configuration","Add Prometheus histogram and labeled counter metrics","Add circuit breaker configuration (error threshold, window, recovery)","Separate liveness and readiness probe configuration in k8s manifests"],"infra_provisioning":["Grafana dashboard for the new metrics","Alert rules for error rate > 5%, p99 latency > 2s, circuit breaker open"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Stateless service, no database."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Cannot compute error rate or measure latency — the two most fundamental SLIs for any API service","reason":"validation_requests_total has no status label and no latency histogram. There is literally no way to set an SLO, create a meaningful alert, or answer 'is this service healthy right now?' from metrics. This is not a nice-to-have; it is table stakes for production.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No circuit breaker or retry logic for sole external dependency","reason":"SmartyStreets is the only path to fulfill a request. Without a circuit breaker, a SmartyStreets brownout will pin all request threads at 3s timeout, exhausting the connection pool and cascading failure to callers. Without retries, transient 5xx errors that would self-heal become caller-visible failures.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No request ID, no input logging, no upstream response logging — zero forensic capability","reason":"When a customer says 'you told me my address was invalid and it's not,' the team cannot pull the request, see what was sent to SmartyStreets, or see what came back. This is a support and debugging dead end.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Health check design will cause self-inflicted outage during upstream degradation","reason":"Liveness probe hitting SmartyStreets means k8s will restart pods when SmartyStreets is down, turning a partial degradation (upstream down, could serve cached/fallback) into total service loss. Must separate liveness from readiness.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability gaps are clear and unambiguous","Resilience gaps (no circuit breaker, no retry) are well-understood failure patterns","Health check anti-pattern is a known k8s operational risk"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact deployment topology unknown — connection pool exhaustion severity depends on concurrency model","Input validation behavior unknown — may already exist but wasn't mentioned","Whether callers have their own timeout/retry that would compound with this service's behavior"],"assumptions":["Service runs in Kubernetes based on health check and rollback-via-image description","No caching layer exists between this service and SmartyStreets","Single SmartyStreets API key with no secondary provider fallback","Structured logging is not already in place (only string-based log lines described)"]}}
